events.text: Gary Martinez, 62-year-old Black male, Current smoker (0.5 ppd). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the Right kidney. Clinical staging: T3bN0M1, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C64.9. Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma with lung metastases.
Bone scan performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Underwent cytoreductive nephrectomy: Right radical nephrectomy with adrenalectomy. Estimated blood loss 150 mL. Length of stay 3 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 immune-related thyroiditis. TSH elevated to 12.5. Levothyroxine 50 mcg daily started.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 hypertension. Amlodipine 5 mg daily initiated. Blood pressure monitoring twice daily.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for restaging after first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination.
Initiated second-line immunotherapy-TKI: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w + Cabozantinib 40 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line immunotherapy-TKI.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line immunotherapy-TKI.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging on second-line immunotherapy-TKI.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Bone scan performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Bone scan performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Bone scan performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
BONE SCAN REPORT

Patient: Gary Martinez
MRN: patient_d7dcef01b9fd
Date of Study: 2023-10-28

CLINICAL INDICATION: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body bone scintigraphy performed following IV injection of 25 mCi Tc-99m MDP.

FINDINGS:
No scintigraphic evidence of osseous metastatic disease. Degenerative uptake in bilateral knees.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right renal mass consistent with renal cell carcinoma. Pulmonary metastases.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Gary Martinez
MRN: patient_d7dcef01b9fd
Specimen ID: S2023-72870
Date of Procedure: 2023-10-30
Date of Report: 2023-11-02

SPECIMEN: Right kidney, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 7.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
CA-IX: Positive (diffuse membranous)
CD10: Positive
CK7: Negative
Vimentin: Positive

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Gary Martinez - Right kidney" is a firm tan tissue fragment measuring 7.2 x 5.0 x 3.6 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A11.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Gary Martinez
MRN: patient_d7dcef01b9fd
Date: 2023-11-06
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Martinez is a 62-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney, IV. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma with lung metastases. Pathology confirmed clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports incidental finding on routine screening.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- peripheral vascular disease

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right knee arthroscopy (age 45)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 5 years ago)
- Alcohol: Rare alcohol use
- Occupation: Retired teacher
- Lives with alone

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Sister with ovarian cancer at age 55.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: unintentional weight loss, night sweats, mild dyspnea on exertion. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 143/83, HR 86, RR 15, Temp 98.4F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 58.0 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 5.8, Hgb 9.2, Plt 160, Cr 1.03

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
62-year-old male with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. initial oncology consultation.

1. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend cytoreductive nephrectomy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Gary Martinez
MRN: patient_d7dcef01b9fd
Date of Report: 2023-11-20
Specimen: Right kidney (Core needle biopsy)
Diagnosis: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3
Panel: Tempus xT (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 4.5 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  VHL p.L89P (c.266T>C) - Missense (Exon 1) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PBRM1 p.Q1298* - Nonsense (Exon 24)
  SETD2 p.R1740* - Nonsense (Exon 11)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  KDM5C p.A388T - VUS
  MTOR p.S2215Y - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - VHL mutation: Consistent with clear cell histology. HIF-pathway targeted therapies (TKIs) and immunotherapy combinations recommended.
  - BAP1 loss: Associated with more aggressive biology.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 723x coverage.
Tumor content: 33%.

Electronically signed by: Katherine Liu, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Gary Martinez
MRN: patient_d7dcef01b9fd
Specimen ID: S2023-32723
Date of Procedure: 2023-12-01
Date of Report: 2023-12-05

SPECIMEN: Right kidney, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 4.9 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
CA-IX: Positive (diffuse membranous)
CD10: Positive
CK7: Negative
Vimentin: Positive

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Gary Martinez - Right kidney" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 4.9 x 3.4 x 2.5 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A5.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

MRN: patient_d7dcef01b9fd
Date: 2023-12-14
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: postoperative follow-up

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Martinez is a 62-year-old male with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney who presents for postoperative follow-up. Patient reports feeling well overall. Appetite is decreased but maintaining weight.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: night sweats, joint pain, dry mouth, constipation. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 110/71, HR 65, RR 18, Temp 97.5F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 88.3 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 8.0, Hgb 12.3, Plt 305, Cr 1.17

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
62-year-old male with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. postoperative follow-up.

1. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 - postoperative follow-up
   - Proceed with next cycle as planned
   - Will check labs prior to next cycle
2. Symptom management
   - Neuropathy being monitored
3. Follow-up in 2 weeks

Electronically signed by: Carlos Mendez, MD
Medical Oncology

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Gary Martinez
MRN: patient_d7dcef01b9fd
Date of Study: 2024-07-15

CLINICAL INDICATION: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. restaging on second-line immunotherapy-TKI.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 0.9 cm in the right upper lobe. Consistent with metastatic disease.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Heterogeneously enhancing mass arising from the right kidney measuring 7.1 x 5.7 cm with extension into the renal vein.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right renal mass consistent with renal cell carcinoma. Pulmonary metastases.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology
